message:
test(harness): conformance ext4 extent block CRC32C tails vs e2fsck

New harness ext4_extent_block_csum_kernel_reference forces a depth-1
extent tree by writing 24 padding files, deleting every other one to
shred the free-block list into 16-KiB holes, then writing a 192-KiB
target file across the resulting fragmented region. mkfs.ext4 1.47.x
reliably produces an external extent leaf block for that target with
ffs-ondisk emitting the expected (depth=1, ≥2 leaf extents).

For every external (non-root) extent block reachable from the target
inode the test recomputes the CRC32C tail via
ffs_ondisk::verify_extent_block_checksum and asserts it matches the
on-disk value mkfs/debugfs wrote. As an independent witness the test
then runs `e2fsck -fn` and asserts a clean (exit-0) verdict — covering
the case where ffs and the kernel might both compute the same wrong
seed but disagree with e2fsprogs. Existing kernel_reference covers
logical→physical mappings only; tail checksums had no end-to-end
conformance pin.

Self-skips when ext4 userland is unavailable. Also adds an
`#[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]` to the prior round's
ext4_group_desc compare_image after rustfmt re-flowed the file long
enough to trip pedantic clippy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
